prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of pyridine was computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard, no density fitting) in the gas phase with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. The calculation employed PySCF (RKS for the neutral state, UKS for the cation and anion states with multiplicities 2) to compute Mulliken partial charges at three charge states (neutral, N−1 cation, and N+1 anion), from which the Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor were derived. All three SCF calculations converged, and the charge-conservation checks passed (∑f⁺ = 1.0000, ∑f⁻ = 1.0000). The most electrophilic site is the nitrogen atom (f⁺ = 0.4842), and the most nucleophilic sites are the para-carbon atoms (f⁻ = 0.1183). A PNG bar chart visualization was generated. Input geometry: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz (11 atoms, used as-is). No warnings reported.
